plugin_desc: TsnTec 8148SC switch-specific SNMP collection (enterprise PEN 65531). Beyond the shared Switch SNMP floor, it collects vendor health OIDs for control-plane CPU usage, memory usage, CPU temperature, fan state and power-supply state. CPU and memory are reported directly as 0-100 percentages. Temperature is read as degrees Celsius from the confirmed CPU temperature scalar. Fan and PSU raw status values are normalized through telegraf processors.enum to 1=healthy / 2=fault, and the worst component state is surfaced per device. The shared Switch floor supplies uptime, IF-MIB interface metrics, 64-bit ifHCInOctets/ifHCOutOctets and aggregate traffic roll-ups.
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the TsnTec switch control-plane CPU utilization from PEN 65531, as a direct 0-100 percentage. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, excessive management polling or abnormal traffic pressure.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports TsnTec system memory utilization from PEN 65531 as a direct 0-100 percentage. High memory usage may indicate oversized forwarding tables, memory leaks or insufficient capacity.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the TsnTec CPU temperature in degrees Celsius from PEN 65531. High temperature indicates thermal risk and can precede throttling, instability or hardware damage.

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: This metric reports TsnTec fan health normalized to 1 = healthy and 2 = fault. A value greater than 1 indicates fan failure or an unknown fan condition requiring inspection.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports TsnTec power-supply health normalized to 1 = healthy and 2 = fault. A value greater than 1 indicates a failed or abnormal power input.
